PHASE: **(auth-bypass-for-e2e) SHIPPED 2026-05-23 AM on `feat/auth-bypass-for-e2e`** — pre-merge. One-time ~280 LOC investment (130 module + 140 tests + 18 wiring) that permanently unblocks Playwright smoke testing per [[playwright-smoke-auth-configuration-blocker]]. Env-gated NextAuth Credentials provider with dual-guard `isE2EBypassEnabled(env)` predicate: requires `AUTH_BYPASS_FOR_E2E === true` AND `NODE_ENV !== "production"`. Provider id `"e2e-bypass"` distinct from real `"credentials"` so prod login UI never routes to it even if bypass code is loaded. `authorizeE2EBypass(creds, db)` is a pure function (db parameterized) accepting only `{ email }` — skips bcrypt + Turnstile but preserves three defense-in-depth checks (status === "active" filtered in query; non-suspended organization; single-match-by-email so cross-org email collisions reject the lookup). Same return shape as real `authorize()` so JWT + session callbacks treat the bypass session identically — same securityVersion staleness check, same RBAC propagation. Locked as 🔴 [[auth-bypass-prod-guard]] gotcha in lessons.md head: never collapse the dual gate to a single condition; never trust a single source of "is this prod?" truth (NODE_ENV is locked to "production" by Vercel + Komodo deploy + `next build`; project-specific env vars are easier to spoof). Stage 1 spec PASS — dual-gate locked by 4 predicate tests covering all NODE_ENV combinations; return shape locked by exact `.toEqual` match; defense-in-depth locked by 3 rejection tests (missing email, no match, suspended org, ambiguous email). Stage 2 quality PASS — zero `any` types (one documented `as unknown` cast at provider factory call site explaining structural-superset safety); TDD RED→GREEN evidenced (initial run failed all 9 cases with "Cannot find package '@/server/auth-bypass'", post-impl run passes 9/9); blast radius = 4 files matching plan exactly (2 NEW source + 2 modified, plus 3 governance); conventional commit format ready; helper is single-responsibility; no shared code with real `authorize()` (deliberate security separation — any future change to real authorize must NOT silently propagate to bypass). 287/287 ✓ (278 → 287, +9 new tests). pnpm lint ✓ 0 errors / typecheck ✓ 0 errors across 8 packages / build ✓ Middleware 141 kB UNCHANGED (Edge surface stable — bypass code is Node-only, never crosses into Edge bundle, consistent with [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]] discipline) / audit --audit-level=critical ✓ exit 0 (1 HIGH pre-documented per [[nodemailer-cve-mitigation]]). Inline Opus 4.7 controller (Tier 1; ~50K context — well within 80K SAFE zone). Skipped vercel-plugin auto-suggestions throughout: bootstrap / auth / next-forge (false positives on auth.ts basename pattern — Vercel Marketplace integrations and starter templates unrelated to custom Auth.js Credentials providers); next-cache-components (false-trigger on "ppr" phrase similarity — actual phrase never appeared in prompt, auth provider work has zero caching surface) per Rule 28 + established skip-list precedent (Yelli runs Next.js 15.5.18, not 16, so `proxy.ts` migration deferred to a future `next-upgrade` ticket).
